entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Screen Personas - Field cannot be moved
entry.content: Aaah, is there no way of doing a comment with "row break"? When I press enter the comment is posted! Sorry for that.. We have however a couple of more things like the button in menus that are buggy and yes. Will try to put in some tickets on that when our release is done. Right now focus is on delivering in project.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi @Shilpa_Shankar ,

I have almost complete this task but facing issue in point c.

Please advise.

entry.title: Re: Express Document "update was cancelled" received from...
entry.content:
Hello @SUSHABHAN_MM 

The reason is that you care a material with automatic numbering. The system fetches next number from a number range assigned to the material type and tries to save the material with that number. However there is already another material created with the same number. Hence the dump. 

You need to correct the number range and at least modify its status.

This kind of errors occurs mostly due to number range transports, as such transport import also number range status to the destination system. That's why transporting number ranges is not automatic and is discouraged. Nevertheless, I think it's ok to transport them with care.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Validation and Substitution rules
entry.content:
Thank you sharing the knowledge. 

In HANA Public cloud , how we can this precondition ( Sum ) For .eg sum of total amount ?

Pls help us

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Document Management: DocuSign and SAP Sales Cloud V2 Integration via BTP
entry.content:
Hello Prasad, @pvsbprasad

Very detailed and helpful usecase blog 👍🏻

Kunal

entry.title: 回复： Custom Entity does not display all line items
entry.content:
Hi, you can try it. you must return the total counts, then use 'UP TO XXX ROWS OFFSET XXXX' return partial data for each requests.

DATA(lo_paging)  = io_request->get_paging( ).
DATA(lv_offset)  = lo_paging->get_offset( ).
DATA(lv_rows)    = lo_paging->get_page_size( ).
DATA(lv_max_rows) = COND #( WHEN lv_rows = 
              if_rap_query_paging=>page_size_unlimited THEN 0 ELSE lv_rows ).


IF io_request->is_total_numb_of_rec_requested( ) = abap_true.
      SELECT COUNT( * ) FROM i_maintenanceordertp WHERE (lv_filter) INTO @DATA(Lv_counts).
      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( Lv_counts ).
ENDIF.
SELECT * FROM i_maintenanceordertp WHERE (lv_filter)
      ORDER BY (lv_orderby_string) INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items)
          UP TO @LV_max_rows ROWS OFFSET @LV_offset.
io_response->set_data( lt_items ).

entry.title: Re: OEE Trends Report in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hello Harsh,

you can fetch the OEE relevant data via public APIs on Business Accelerator Hub, see https://api.sap.com/api/sapdmi_vishleshki/overview

There is no API on the real-time KPIs being calculated.

HANA DB Live Connection with 2408 is READ-ONLY access. There is no support for creating custom stored procedure or anything similar.

We are planning to provide an OEE MDO based on which you will be able to calculate and expose OEE trend in one of the subsequent releases for SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Thanks,

Marcel

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content: Great news, thanks for the update  👍

entry.title: Re: Analytical Table not showing criticality
entry.content: Thanks Chistopher, had same issue when using aggregations in a grid table and adding RequestAtLeast works for me. Funny thing is that the grid table without aggegrations worked, but it broke when adding SUM aggegrations.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi @diveshkalra ,

The task is to add 'Status text' field in the filter bar. As it has only 3 values we need to define an annotation which makes it is a value help where the values will be displayed and can be filtered on. 

Hint: Check the Annotation TechByte video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GXFHjq5L8M8&t=1030s

entry.title: Re: How to maintain the entries of the table TCOBM with a report
entry.content: Hi Lionel, is there any solution to the above problem？Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Incoming Payment Issue: No transaction type coulde be...
entry.content:
Solution: after talking to the bank to provide previous bank files and test them in the QAS we noticed the new incoming payment file contains one new item 'Fast Payment' which is not included in our customizing so therefore this was not able to load. We requested the bank to remove this new line while we need to work on the mapping and updating the payment customizing with the missing part which is newly added by the local bank. Hope this will help all in future. 

<Ntry>

VCOMK

<NtryDtls>

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi @Chandana_Pamarthi ,

Thanks for the submission. For better readability you can filter out on the Status Text instead of the Status Code.

Regards,

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

@David_Chaviano 

Hello David,

I fall under below two category and wanted to switch my career in SAP.

  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

entry.title: Re: Unable to deploy iFlow in Integration Suite for Azure...
entry.content: In the Connection tab, the Alias should reference the credential (secure parameter) for the SAS token you created before, not the actual SAS token.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi Gaurav,

Thanks for the submission. Could you add search field and enable Description to be searched.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi @Shilpa_Shankar ,

I tried same way by following the youtube link but not sure where it's going wrong.

Below is the code:

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'CDS View entity'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

@ObjectModel.usageType:{

serviceQuality: #X,

sizeCategory: #S,

dataClass: #MIXED

}

@UI.headerInfo.typeName: 'Travel'

@UI.headerInfo.typeNamePlural: 'Travels'

@Search.searchable: true

@ObjectModel.semanticKey: [ 'Description' ]

 

define view entity Ztravel_entity5 as select from ztravel_new5

association [0..1] to /DMO/I_Overall_Status_VH as _Status on _Status.OverallStatus = $projection.Status

{

@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]

@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

key

travel_id as TravelId,

@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]

@Search.defaultSearchElement: true

@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.90

description as Description,

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'

@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]

total_price as TotalPrice,

currency_code as CurrencyCode,

@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]

concat_with_space( cast ( total_price as abap.char(25) ), currency_code, 1) as NewPriceCurrencyCode,

@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]

status as Status,

@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]

@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: {name: 'zhelp_new5', element: 'overallStatus' } }]

_Status._Text.Text as overallStatusText,

_Status

 

}

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024

We eventually managed to get the upload to work but seems to be very susceptible to the period/date selection that one chooses.

  1. Works with a calendar date
  2. Does not seem to work with a financial period

Regards

Derek

entry.title: Re: Fiori App F2359 Error: Target was not found
entry.content: That worked, thanks!

entry.title: Fixed Asset Master data and capitalization for the depreciated machine
entry.content: I am having trouble to enroll the old machine which have been depreciated for the last 7 years to Fixed asset master data in SAP B1 for new customer.   The machine has 8 Years useful life in depreciation plan.  And my customer started use SAP B1 from this year.   Do i have to make the fiscal period from 2016 (7 years ago) for the capitalization for this machine ?    Most of materials and You tube lecture for SAP B1 FA is about new machine purchase and capitalization, not the depreciated one.      Could anyone give me some guide for this case ?

entry.content:
Hi @AVG ,

Thanks for the submission. For better readability you can filter out on the Status Text instead of the Status Code.

Regards,

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: SSCUI 102139 to define DME user ID is unavailable.
entry.content: Dear customer, we are working on resolving the issue. If you need to change your customizing in the meantime please open an incident to FI-AP-AP-B1 component. Kind regards,

entry.title: Generate single PDF of image collection stored in a Folder
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have folder on my system which has n number of images. What I want is to generate a single pdf which contains these images. I have made a service in SAP BAS but what I want is that I will pass my folder path using which the service will generate a single pdf of these images stored in this folder path.

How do i do it using UI5 and CAPM or any other method present in it.

Regards,

Ayush

entry.title: ALM Nuggets: Creation of Public Views on SAP BTP for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Introduction

With this blogpost I will describe the new possibility to create public views, which can be used by all users within your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Views

You find views in mostly every Apps in SAP Cloud ALM on the top left side.

Steps on the SAP BTP Cockpit

Open your SAP BTP cockpit:
https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Open the subaccount that contains your SAP Cloud ALM tenant:
Choose Security  > Role Collections > Create 

Name: Public View Editor
Description: Create and edit public views, which are shared with all users in your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Under Roles, select the role FlexPublicViewEditor and choose Add and click on Save.

 

Under Users, select all users who should be able to create public views and click on Save.

SAP Cloud ALM

It is now possible to create public views.
And every SAP Cloud ALM user can use the public views.

In the user management of SAP Cloud ALM you can now also see the new role:

That's it.

entry.title: Re: CAP - CDS REPL to connect to db {kind : hana}
entry.content:
Hello Dinu,

Thank you for your response. I tried the said command, however I still get errors with cds.test() and also with await cds.connect.to('db').

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to use own IdP User for SAP HANA CLOUD...
entry.content:
I'm not sure if I understood your need correctly, but if you want to give access to the HANA Cloud Central and HANA Cloud DB Explorer to the users defined in the iDp, then it is very simple - all you need to do is to setup your IAS tenant, connect your IdP to the IAS, establish trust between HANA Cloud subaccount and IAS and map IAS user groups to BTP subaccount role collections:

1. Setup your IAS,

2. Connect your IdP to your IAS,

3. Go to BTP cockpit,

4. Go to your HANA Cloud subaccount,

5. Go to "Security" -> "Trust Configuration",

6. Establish trust with your IAS,

7. Map IAS HANA Cloud users group to HANA Cloud role collections in the HANA Cloud subaccount

That's all.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to use own IdP User for SAP HANA CLOUD...
entry.content: Ok, thanks for the clarification. This is not supported apart from LDAP at this point in time indeed, but is on the roadmap for non-LDAP (using Identity Provisioning): https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800002881&q=provision&range=FIRST-LAST#;INNO=1FB6910EE2771EEF81E157160E68BC79

entry.title: Tools for Activate Methodology
entry.content:
Hi, 

 I want to know anyone using various tools/suites available from SAP for ALM i.e. signavio, solution manager, road map viewer. In practice I have seen various implementations where they follow their own or third party tools i.e. Visio, MS Project, Jira, Asana. Any champion can guide why SAP tools are not fully utilized by the implementers or organization which are transforming to SAP?

Also guide me as a beginner how to adopt to using SAP tools as various teams are already experienced on other tools.

Please share if you have any knowledge on this.

Kind regards.

Kaya Ercan

entry.title: SAPGUI not connect
entry.content: SAPGUI does not connect to SAP cloud s4hana.

entry.title: ALM Nuggets: Erstellen öffentlicher Ansichten auf der SAP BTP für SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Einführung

In diesem Blogbeitrag beschreibe ich die neue Möglichkeit, öffentliche Ansichten zu erstellen, die von allen Benutzern in Ihrem SAP Cloud ALM tenant verwendet werden können.

Ansichten

Sie finden Ansichten in fast allen Apps in SAP Cloud ALM oben links.

Schritte im SAP BTP Cockpit

Öffnen Sie das SAP BTP Cockpit:
https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Öffnen Sie den Subaccount für Ihren SAP Cloud ALM tenant:
Wählen Sie: Security  > Role Collections > Create 

Name: Public View Editor
Description: Create and edit public views, which are shared with all users in your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Unter Roles, selektieren Sie die Rolle FlexPublicViewEditor und fügen diese hinzu.

Unter Users wählen Sie diejenigen Benutzer aus welche öffentliche Ansichten erstellen dürfen.

SAP Cloud ALM

Es ist jetzt möglich, öffentliche Ansichten zu erstellen.
Und jeder SAP Cloud ALM Benutzer kann die öffentlichen Ansichten verwenden.

Im User Management von SAP Cloud ALM können sie die neue Rolle nun ebenfalls sehen:

Das wars, vielen Dank fürs lesen.

entry.title: 订单结算报错消息号 MLCCS009
entry.content:
KO88订单结算时报错，消息号MLCCS009
 
发送者：ORD 3300000386 收球筒橇 P7.5MPa DN100
在 LCL_ACTIVITY_COST_SETTLEMENT 方法 CHECK_AND_RESCALE 头寸 3 中 RC 内部错 误 消息编号 MLCCS009

entry.title: (German) Der geheime Karriere-Boost: Warum Sie eine SAP Signavio Zertifizierung brauchen!
entry.content:
Blog post in English

Wie würde es sich anfühlen, wenn Sie sich zertifizierter "Process Management Consultant" oder "Process Data Analyst" nennen könnten? Und dabei noch Chancen haben, Ihr Gehalt zu steigern und Ihre Karriere voranzubringen?

Genau das habe ich in diesem Monat erreicht! Also... nicht mein Gehalt zu steigern oder meine Karriere voranzubringen. Ich gebe zu, das war ein wenig clickbaity, um Ihre Aufmerksamkeit zu gewinnen 😄 (wobei ehrlich gesagt, so ganz weit hergeholt ist das nicht! Sie erfahren gleich noch, was es damit auf sich hat).

Zurück zum Anfang: Ich darf mich nun tatsächlich ein von SAP zertifizierter "Process Management Consultant" oder "Process Data Analyst" nennen (Hier können Sie übrigens meine erworbenen Zertifizierungen einsehen 😃).

Meine Erfahrungen mit diesen Zertifizierungen möchte ich gerne mit Ihnen teilen, und vor allem, was Sie persönlich davon haben.

 

Was sind SAP Zertifizierungen?

Zunächst einmal… SAP Zertifizierungen – was ist das genau? SAP Zertifizierungen sind offizielle Nachweise über spezifische SAP-Kenntnisse einer Person. Sie dienen dazu, die Kompetenz im Umgang mit SAP-Lösungen zu belegen und das berufliche Profil aufzuwerten.

Es gibt verschiedene SAP Zertifizierungen für unterschiedliche Lösungen und Schwierigkeitsstufen. Manche sind für Einsteiger:innen gedacht, andere für Leute, die schon ein bisschen mehr Erfahrung mitbringen.

Wenn Sie eine solche Zertifizierung in der Tasche haben, zeigen Sie damit: „Hey, ich kenne mich aus, ich weiß, was ich tue.“ Und das sieht nicht nur auf Ihrem Lebenslauf gut aus – es kann auch Türen öffnen, sei es für eine neue Jobchance, eine Gehaltserhöhung oder einfach nur mehr Sicherheit in dem, was Sie tun.

SAP Zertifizierungen gibt es in vielen verschiedenen Bereichen, zum Beispiel für Finanzmodule, Vertriebsprozesse, Logistik oder Programmierung. Oder eben SAP Signavio, worüber ich mehr berichten werde.

Sie können sich also genau in dem Bereich zertifizieren lassen, der für Ihre Karriere am besten passt. Und keine Sorge, Sie müssen nicht gleich ein SAP-Guru sein, um anzufangen. Es gibt Einstiegsmöglichkeiten, die Ihnen Schritt für Schritt alles beibringen.

 

Warum sollten Sie sich zertifizieren lassen?

Manch eine:r ist süchtig nach Abschlüssen und Bescheinigungen, aus welchem Grund auch immer. Um den eigenen Selbstwert zu erhöhen oder sich auf LinkedIn zur Schau zu stellen, ich weiß nicht. Sorry für alle anderen, aber das ist, was ich in meinem 29-jährigen Leben häufig beobachtet habe.

Das sollte meiner Meinung nach nicht die Motivation sein, eine SAP Zertifizierung zu erhalten. Warum aber dann? Was spricht sonst dafür, sich ein offizielles Zertifikat von einem renommierten Unternehmen wie die SAP ausstellen zu lassen, dass man Expert:in auf einem speziellen Gebiet ist?

 

SAP ist das technologische Herzstück vieler Unternehmen. Wenn Sie wissen, wie man SAP richtig nutzt, dann werden Sie unersetzlich. Unternehmen suchen händeringend nach Leuten, die sich damit auskennen, und mit einer SAP Zertifizierung zeigen Sie nicht nur, dass Sie das Zeug dazu haben, sondern auch, dass Sie wirklich Expertenwissen mitbringen. Das bringt Ihnen einen großen Vorteil auf dem Arbeitsmarkt.

Besonders hilfreich ist sie bei Anfragen für ein Beratungsprojekt oder Bewerbungen auf einen neuen Job. Deshalb ist meine anfängliche Clickbait-Aussage, “Chancen zu haben, Ihr Gehalt zu steigern und Ihre Karriere voranzubringen”, gar nicht so weit hergeholt. Sie können schwarz auf weiß vorweisen, dass Sie sich mit einer SAP-Lösung auskennen und ein gewisses Level an Expertise mitbringen.

Und – ich gebe zu – hier kann es tatsächlich von Vorteil sein, auf LinkedIn zur Schau zu stellen, dass Sie solche Zertifizierungen besitzen.

 

Aber es geht nicht nur um Jobs und Gehälter – auch wenn das schon ziemlich gut klingt, oder? Eine SAP Zertifizierung gibt Ihnen Selbstbewusstsein. Sie wissen, dass Sie etwas können, das wirklich wertvoll ist. Und dieses Wissen, dieser „Hey, ich hab das drauf“-Moment, gibt Ihnen ein großartiges Gefühl.

Die Zertifierungsprüfung ist eine Challenge an sich selbst: “Kenne ich mich wirklich auf dem Gebiet aus oder habe ich doch noch riesige Wissenslücken? Habe ich verstanden, was ich in einem Kurs über mehrere Tage oder Wochen hinweg gelernt habe?”

Ja, eine SAP Zertifizierung ist anspruchsvoll – aber genau das ist ja das Gute daran! Sie fordern sich selbst heraus, erweitern Ihre Komfortzone und wachsen über sich hinaus.

Letztlich macht eine SAP Zertifizierung Sie nicht nur fit für den Jobmarkt, sondern sorgt auch dafür, dass Sie sich selbstbewusst und kompetent fühlen.

 

Also, habe ich Sie genug motiviert, sich nun für SAP Signavio zertifizieren zu lassen?

 

Welche Zertifizierungen gibt es für SAP Signavio?

Am Anfang habe ich mit den tollen Bezeichnungen geprahlt, wie ich mich nun nennen darf 😉. Diese Betitelung habe ich aus den Namen der beiden Zertifierungen entnommen, welche die SAP für SAP Signavio anbietet:

SAP Certified Associate - Process Management Consultant - SAP Signavio

Diese Zertifizierung richtet sich an Expert:innen, die mit SAP Signavio Lösungen Geschäftsprozesse managen. Sie bescheinigt, dass Sie über grundlegende Kenntnisse verfügt, um Geschäftsprozesse zu managen und zu designen – stets mit dem bzw. der Kund:in im Fokus.

SAP Certified Associate - Process Data Analyst - SAP Signavio

Diese Zertifizierung richtet sich an Expert:innen, die Arbeitsabläufe mit Hilfe von Prozessdaten unter die Lupe nehmen und verbessern möchten. Sie bescheinigt, dass Sie über die nötigen Grundkenntnisse verfügen, um gründliche Analysen vorzunehmen und Optimierungsvorschläge zu erarbeiten.

 

SAP Signavio Learning Journeys – die perfekte Vorbereitung auf Ihre Zertifizierung

SAP Zertifizierungen haben ein so hohes Ansehen, weil sie nicht mal nebenbei gemacht werden und kinderleichte Aufgaben beinhalten, die man im Schlaf beantworten könnte.

Nein, im Gegenteil!

Viele der Fragen in der Zertifizierungsprüfungen haben es wirklich in sich und bedürfen einer umfassenden Vorbereitung, um nicht wie der Ochs vorm Berg zu stehen.

Aber keine Angst, es ist alles machbar. Ich selbst habe beide Zertifizierungen beim ersten Durchgang geschafft, mit gar nicht mal soo schlechtem Ergebnis… 😀

Für jede der Zertifizierungen habe ich mir mehrere Tage ganz viel Zeit genommen, um die kostenlosen Learning Journeys durchzuarbeiten, mir eine lange Mitschrift zu machen und zu lernen. Genauso, wie damals zu Uni-Zeiten.

Und das hat gereicht! Ich persönlich brauchte nicht weniger Vorbereitung, aber auch nicht mehr.

 

Für die Zertifizierung zum Process Management Consultant habe ich die Learning Journey Managing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions durchgearbeitet. Sie erhalten darin eine Einführung allgemein ins Thema Business Process Management, Prozess-Modellierung und Zusammenarbeit.

Natürlich bringt der Kurs auch bei, wie Sie mit einzelnen SAP Signavio Lösungen wie dem Process Manager, Collaboration Hub, Process Governance und Journey Modeler arbeiten. Eine extra Einheit richtet sich speziell an Administrator:innen.

 

Für die andere Zertifizierung zum Process Data Analysten stand die Learning Journey Analyzing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions auf meinem Lehrplan. Ganz wenige Themen haben sich überschnitten mit dem, was ich bereits zuvor gelernt hatte, nämlich die allgemeine Einführung ins Business Process Management und die Kollaboration.

Dann ging es aber auch schon direkt weiter mit Process Mining, der Arbeit mit Process Intelligence als Analyst:in, und vorbereitende Maßnahmen zum Extrahieren, Transformieren und Laden der Daten.

Nachdem Sie die mächtige Datenabfrage-Srache “SiGNAL” kennengelernt haben, folgt eine Einführung in Process Insights mit einem besonderen Fokus auf Guided Analysis.

Abschließend erfahren Sie ausführlich, was es mit dem wunderbaren Plug-and-Gain-Ansatz auf sich hat.

 

Jetzt durchstarten mit Ihrer SAP Signavio Zertifizierung!

Wenn Sie Lust haben, Ihre SAP Signavio-Karriere auf das nächste Level zu bringen und sich mit einer Zertifizierung einen echten Vorteil zu verschaffen, dann sollten Sie sich unbedingt weiter damit zu beschäftigen. Es ist eine zeitliche (und vielleicht finanzielle) Investition in sich selbst, die sich definitiv auszahlt – und das fühlt sich hervorragend an!

Also, das sind die nächsten Schritte für Sie:

  1. Besprechen Sie mit Ihrem Chef bzw. Ihrer Chefin, dass Sie vorhaben, sich zertifizieren zu lassen. Womöglich können Sie es sogar als offizielles Jahresziel eintragen.
  2. Nehmen Sie sich einige Tage Zeit (oder einige Stunden über mehrere Wochen) und arbeiten die kostenlose Learning Journey durch.
  3. Wenn Sie sich fit fühlen, reservieren Sie einen Termin für die Zertifizierungsprüfung

 

Viel Erfolg Ihnen!

Ihr Dennis Streichert

entry.title: Re: Displaying the single/ multiple Customized Header Messages at HTTP Response under Success Status
entry.content:
Hi Sreeramg,

Greetings

Could you help us getting the http status code as 200 in post method.

We have redefined the create deep entity.

entry.title: The Hidden Career Boost: Why You Need an SAP Signavio Certification!
entry.content:
Blog post in German

How would it feel to call yourself a certified "Process Management Consultant" or "Process Data Analyst"? And at the same time, have the chance to increase your salary and advance your career?

That’s exactly what I achieved this month! Well... not increasing my salary or advancing my career. I admit, that was a bit clickbaity to get your attention 😊 (although, honestly, it's not that far-fetched! You’ll find out what I mean in a moment).

Back to the beginning: I can now indeed call myself an SAP certified "Process Management Consultant" or "Process Data Analyst." (By the way, you can view my certifications here 😃).

I’d like to share my experiences with these certifications with you, and most importantly, explain what you personally can gain from them.

 

What are SAP Certifications?

First of all… SAP certifications – what exactly are they? SAP certifications are official proofs of specific SAP knowledge. They serve to demonstrate competence in using SAP solutions and to enhance one’s professional profile.

There are various SAP certifications for different solutions and difficulty levels. Some are intended for beginners, while others are for people with a bit more experience.

If you own a certification, it shows: "Hey, I know what I'm doing." And that not only looks good on your resume – it can also open doors, be it for a new job opportunity, a salary increase, or simply more confidence in what you do.

SAP certifications are available in many different areas, for example, finance modules, sales processes, logistics, or programming. Or, specifically, SAP Signavio, which I will talk about in more detail.

You can get certified in the area that best suits your career. And don’t worry, you don’t have to be an SAP guru to start. There are entry-level options that teach you everything step by step.

 

Why Should You Get Certified?

Some people are addicted to degrees and certificates, for whatever reason. To boost their self-esteem or show off on LinkedIn, I don’t know. Sorry to everyone else, but that’s what I’ve often observed in my 29 years of life.

In my opinion, that shouldn’t be the motivation to get an SAP certification. But why then? What else speaks in favor of getting an official certificate from a reputable company like SAP, certifying that you are an expert in a specific field?

 

SAP is the technological heart of many companies. If you know how to use SAP correctly, you become indispensable. Companies are desperately looking for people who are knowledgeable in SAP, and with an SAP certification, you not only show that you have what it takes, but that you also bring real expert knowledge. This gives you a significant advantage in the job market.

It’s particularly helpful when applying for a consulting project or a new job. That’s why my initial clickbait statement, "having the chance to increase your salary and advance your career," isn’t that far-fetched. You can prove that you know your way around an SAP solution and bring a certain level of expertise.

And – I admit – it can actually be advantageous to showcase on LinkedIn that you possess such certifications.

 

But it’s not just about jobs and salaries – although that does sound pretty good, right? An SAP certification gives you confidence. You know that you are able to work professionally with this solution is truly valuable.

The certification exam is a challenge to yourself: "Do I really know this field, or do I still have huge gaps in my knowledge? Did I understand what I learned in a course over several days or weeks?"

Yes, an SAP certification is demanding – but that’s exactly the good thing about it! You challenge yourself, expand your comfort zone, and grow beyond yourself.

In the end, an SAP certification not only makes you fit for the job market but also ensures that you feel confident and competent.

Well, have I motivated you enough to get certified for SAP Signavio now?

 

What certifications are available for SAP Signavio?

At the beginning, I boasted about the great titles I can now call myself 😊. I took these names from the descriptions of the two certifications that SAP offers for SAP Signavio:

SAP Certified Associate - Process Management Consultant - SAP Signavio

This certification is for experts who manage business processes with SAP Signavio solutions. It proofs that you have the basic knowledge to manage and design business processes – always with the customer in focus.

SAP Certified Associate - Process Data Analyst - SAP Signavio

This certification is for experts who want to analyze and improve processes using process data. It proofs that you have the necessary basic knowledge to conduct thorough analyses and develop optimization suggestions.

 

SAP Signavio Learning Journeys – The Perfect Preparation for Your Certification

SAP certifications have such a high reputation because they aren’t just done on the side with tasks you could answer in your sleep.

No, quite the opposite!

Many of the questions in the certification exams are really tough and require extensive preparation so you don’t end up looking like a deer in headlights.

But don’t worry, it’s all doable. I myself passed both certifications on the first try, with not-so-bad results… 😉

For each of the certifications, I took several days to work through the free Learning Journeys, take extensive notes, and study. Just like back in university days.

And that was enough! Personally, I didn’t need less preparation, but also not more.

 

For the Process Management Consultant certification, I worked through the Learning Journey "Managing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions." It provides a general introduction to Business Process Management, process modeling, and collaboration.

Of course, the course also teaches how to work with individual SAP Signavio solutions such as Process Manager, Collaboration Hub, Process Governance, and Journey Modeler. A special unit is specifically aimed at administrators.

 

For the other certification as a Process Data Analyst, the Learning Journey "Analyzing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions" was on my syllabus. Very few topics overlapped with what I had previously learned, namely the general introduction to Business Process Management and collaboration.

Then it went straight into Process Mining, working with Process Intelligence as an analyst, and preparatory measures for extracting, transforming, and loading data.

After you learn the powerful query language "SiGNAL," there’s an introduction to Process Insights with a special focus on Guided Analysis.

Finally, you’ll get a detailed explanation of what the wonderful Plug-and-Gain approach is all about.

 

Start Now with Your SAP Signavio Certification!

If you’re eager to take your SAP Signavio career to the next level and gain a real advantage with a certification, then you should definitely delve deeper into it. It’s an investment of time (and maybe money) in yourself that definitely pays off – and that feels fantastic!

So, here are the next steps for you:

  1. Discuss with your maanger that you plan to get certified. You might even be able to make it an official annual goal.
  2. Take a few days (or a few hours over several weeks) and work through the free Learning Journey.
  3. When you feel well prepared, book an appointment for the certification exam.

 

May you do well!

Your Dennis Streichert

entry.title: Re: CLM in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Cristian, 

I have never came across this combination so far. In the past these two could be connected in ECC 6.0 by using the Public Sector  (PS-CD) component and activating Collections & Disbursement (PSCD) business function. By activating the business function the necessary integration is ready for configuration.

For using Funds Management as well another business function had to be activated (FICA_FM) but the business function for Enterprise Extension Public Services had to be activated as well.

In the Public Cloud I think that there is no connection (yet) between both.

Best regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Completed!

entry.title: Re: Memory protection fault in OLE container control - Ad...
entry.content: Yes, it got started so suddenly in no time. previously it worked properly.

entry.title: Re: #COE#BUMA - How to disable SAP system notifications for feedback
entry.content:
To assist you better, I might need to ask a few clarifying questions:

1.Which RPA Tool Are You Using?

2.How Are the Notifications Delivered? Are the notifications you're receiving pop-up messages within the SAP GUI, email notifications, or something else?

3.When Do These Notifications Occur? Do these notifications appear at specific times or during particular transactions or processes?

4.Have You Tried Any Suppression Methods?Have you attempted any methods to suppress these notifications, such as modifying user parameters or changing system settings?

5.Impact on Processes:How critical is it to suppress these notifications immediately? Is it causing process failures, delays, or data inconsistencies?

6.Configuration of BOT User.Is the BOT user configured differently from a regular user in terms of authorizations or system settings? or Special configurations might help in fine-tuning system behavior for the BOT.

***Potential Solutions Based on General Use Cases:

  • Suppressing SAP GUI Messages:
  • Use SAP GUI scripting to capture and dismiss unwanted pop-ups.
  • Running in Background Mode:
  • Ensure your BOT processes are scheduled as background jobs, minimizing the chance of encountering interactive prompts.

Feel free to provide additional details or answer the questions above so that I can give more targeted advice!

entry.title: Hiring for SAP SuccessFactors/HCM Consultants & Managers - Big Four - Belgium (Permanent)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am currently working with a Partner for Human Capital with one of the Big Four. They require SuccessFactors Consultants as well as SAP HCM Consultants across various seniorities. Within this business, they offer a clear promotion path into leadership if this is an area you wish to grow into, as well as ample opportunities to obtain certifications and relevant training.

As expected within the Big Four, they work with global customers and large-scale transformation projects, many of which are greenfield implementations of SuccessFactors modules. 

Salaries range from €61,000 to €80,000 + all additional benefits, they are also able to optimize the net salaries well. You must be living in Belgium for these opportunities and speak Dutch or French.

For more information, please email me at alfie.lampe@empiric.com or connect with me on LinkedIn (@alfielampe).

You should find the buttons in the ribbon now.

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

the question is: Did it work before 24.08.0 ? We would need to know when it "stopped" working if you suspect a change on our end cause this. However - either way i would kindly ask you to create a support ticket here and mention that i (Franzisca Zarrai) recommended you create a ticket so that support knows. Also post the link to this conversation here and as much detail as you can provide:

  • How are you trying to use the keyboard/add it to FSM?
  • Did it ever work? If yes with which version of the mobile app did it stop working? Or was it an OS update after which it stopped working?

The more details you provide, the better the odds are that we can help you with this.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Unable to deploy iFlow in Integration Suite for Azure...
entry.content: Ok great, this is more like a security best-practice to not put credentials directly in your flows, but manage them centrally and securely. You will see this concept in multiple places in Integration Suite.

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content: Hi Franzisca, thanks for the reply

entry.title: Re: Configuration Tab missing in PDS screen (/n/SAPAPO/CU...
entry.content:
Hi,

Is the product VC or CDP relevant?
Is the product set as a KMAT or a variant?
You need to ensure that if you have a KMAT that and your use e.g CDP configuration that the CDP class is assigned and the so called flagclass field is set correct.


There are KBAs for that which can help so correct maybe incorrect product master data in PPDS.
see e.g. KBA https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3456991  Wrong Configuration Relevancy on PPDS Product Master in ePPDS or DSC system

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2927766  - Missing class type 230 and 400 on S4HANA PPDS system.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/cdp-class-tab-not-populated-in-product-master-even-though-it-is-maintained/qaq-p/12747972

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content:
Hi Franzisca, thanks for the reply.

I created case 835635/2024: Not possible to add third party keyboard in FSM Mobile App.
Regarding the version, I know it was working around the 23rd of April or something like that, because we were testing third party keyboards then.

Now we created our own numeric keyboard and we are not able to add it to the mobile app.

Kind regards

Laurens

entry.title: Re: Migrating Workflows to Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I have checked the "What's new" section for the workflow management, BPA and Product Lifecycle Management and i see there indeed hasn't been an update on the 16th of August.

But we came across this article Migration Article in which it is stated:
"Starting with the July 2024 delivery of SAP Product Lifecycle Management, it is mandatory for you to migrate from SAP Workflow Management service to SAP Build Process Automation if you want to continue viewing existing workflows, creating new workflows for collaborations."

Just to make sure, my question is: if we want to use only BPA without BAS, is it possible to import old workflows AND update them inside the BPA, or do we have to build new ones in BPA ?

entry.title: Re: How to configure XSUAA in CAP + MDK?
entry.content: Hi Emorales, I would suggest you to port your CAP OData V4 service into a LCAP project in BAS, and also create a MDK UI app in it, and then deploy the project to BTP, LCAP deployment module will take care all the security configuration automatically for you, you can compare the configuration of the LCAP project with the one of your current project to see any differences.

entry.title: When creating a New Cancellation Invoice, the Posting Date field is grayed out.
entry.content:
When we try to reverse few customer invoices the "Posting Date" field in the reversal document cannot be changed. 
For other invoices from a closed posting period, the field still appears to be changeable. Also set the field to editable for invoices from open posting periods.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: When creating a New Cancellation Invoice, the Posting...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

The system behavior is correct since the posting period is currently in status Open and Not blocked.

If the posting period is open and an invoice in that particular period is canceled, the Posting Date field in the new cancellation invoice will be grayed out and read-only.

It assures that the invoice and the cancellation invoice fall into the same accounting period.

To be able to change the posting date, you need to close the period following the steps below:

  1. Go to the General Ledger work center.
  2. Choose the Companies view.
  3. Select the company ID XYZ (XYZ represents the ID of the company)
  4. Click Set Process Control.
  5. Select the relevant Process Control and change the Process Control Status to Blocked.
  6. Click Save.

After that, you are able to change the posting date in the cancellation invoice.

Regards, 

Arjun

entry.title: Infotype 0008: Can I block specific fields for enter information that are related to infotype 0007?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Is it possible to block fields in infotype 0008 to enter information? We experience different information regarding work time between IT0008 and IT0007. People ignore the hint when updating information in IT0007 that the information needs to be changed in IT0008 as well.

Is it either possible to display the information from IT0007 in the view of IT0008 and prevent changes her? Or is there any other good solution? Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards,

Ulrike

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud & SAP Document Management Service, Integration Option - External Repository
entry.content: I set up my S/4HANA Cloud with an internal DMS repo, but when I go to attach a document o a business partner, it says it cannot reach it. Is there any trace or way to know where it failed (e.g., bad credentials, bad repo, maybe it did not even get to calling the API).

entry.title: Re: Input Control and Filtering - Unbooked data
entry.content:
At the moment there is no validation rules between dimensions like you require, but I'm aware of an idea enhancement for this so hopefully it will arrive some day.

In the meantime, one approach is to create an input story (for administrators) on a separate part of the data model (e.g. on a dummy version). You would enter the value of e.g. "1" into the correct combinations of accounts and legal entities. 

Then in your story, you can bring in the dummy version into the 1st column of your table (you may even be able to hide with right click/hide member), and then you set it to booked data only. This should then show all of the combinations you require per legal entity selected.

thank you for your support and experience.
Kr,
Elena

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
@gerd_schoeffl 

Just FYI I've added a new enhancement request for 2 features I believe should be available - clean/replace version and clean/replace subset of data. At the moment with the widget there's no way to clear data debris if a user is uploading data to an area with existing data. This would require a separate data action to do (but we cannot also yet embed file upload starters into multi-actions).

All - please vote on the enhancement request if you would like this feature too!

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/329138

I also agree that the fiscal year setting would be great to have as for some clients this would be needed to be able to use this functionality, plus the master data creation option. 

 

David

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Thanks @comercial_cinfa for the reply. It will be supported in future releases. 

we will evaluate to support the Bookmark options. But after applying filters (data point selection) it will create a new state (similar to bookmark) in PPT and one click of refresh will bring you the latest data.

Regards,

Ashok R

entry.title: Error Update statistics of table failed in ATM Logs (ASE db)
entry.content:
Hello,

I get the following error message for different tables in my ATM logs in the DBACOCKPIT: Update statistics of table failed

The tables are both in saptools and in the area in which the data from the tables in SAP are located. The db is an Sybase ASE db (version: 16.0.04.04). The SAP version is 753 

I couldn't find any noticeable consequences but would like to resolve these errors. Does anyone know why this happens and how I can solve it?

I could only find solutions for this error if the update is executed manually.

Thank you

entry.title: Host Alias in SAP Integration Suite Trial
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently trying to use the SAP Integration Suite Trial to work trough different tutorials. In particular I tried the following two tutorials:

  • https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-with-sap-integration-suite/creating-an-api_b3973a9a-62ce-4a5e-b59a-c0ddf6924de5
  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-apim-create-api.html

The problem with the Integration Suite Trial is, that the Host Alias, which is generated for an API Proxy, can not be changed. At least I didn't find any option to do so. As a result it is not possible to follow the two tutorials mentioned above completely.

I found the following question related to the issue: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-learning-q-a/developing-with-sap-integration-suite-how-to-modify-the-api-proxy-host-name/qaq-p/224504. This post shows that it is possible to invoke the API Proxy by manually adjusting the URL in the test environment.

Is there any way to change the Host Alias for a generated API Proxy in the trail environment?

Christian

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

My name is Soumya Chakraborty, I am a student who's trying to shift his career trajectory and being immensely interested in SAP and after working on a project based on creating a business blueprint for a company for their SAP implementation, I want to become certified with SAP and take on my professional journey. I have recently completed the TS410 course(Integrated Business Process) through the Digital Skills Initiative, which I found incredibly valuable. However, I am in a challenging situation and need your assistance.

Previously, I attempted the certification exam and unfortunately scored 58%, just shy of the required 61% for passing. This has been weighing heavily on me, as I am truly dedicated to advancing my skills and contributing effectively to my career. I am confident that if given another opportunity, I can successfully pass the exam and achieve the certification.

Due to some pressing personal and professional reasons, it is crucial for me to clear this certification at the earliest as my placement for my professional degree in starting soon. I am urgently requesting your support to allow me to retake the exam under the initiative mentioned on the website. I assure you that this time, I will put in the extra effort needed to succeed.

entry.title: Re: Service Layer Single Sign On (SSO) example or tutorial
entry.content:
Hi MDIAS,

The explanation is provided in reference to Service Layer only.

You will not be able to login using SAP Business One user and password when SSO is enabled.

You will have to use the user created in SLD and password in order to login into Service Layer. Same user name and password which you use in SAP Business One client after enabling SSO.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How to Block a Ship-to Location in SAP ByDesign
entry.content: Thank you for your reply. Following your instructions, I blocked the status of the Ship-to Logistics Area and created a Stock Transfer Order. I was able to create the Stock Transfer Order and the subsequent Outbound Delivery, but I couldn't select the blocked Logistics Area when creating the Inbound Delivery. With the 2408 update, isn't it possible to perform a Stock Transfer to a blocked Logistics Area? I have an obligation to verify the details of this update and explain it to the customer. Could you please provide me with step-by-step instructions on how to verify the update, along with addressing the above question? Screenshots Explanation Evidence of blocking the Logistics Area "4IDF02 10" Evidence of creating the Stock Transfer Order and the subsequent Outbound Delivery Evidence showing the error message "Logistics Area '10' cannot be used" when attempting to create the Inbound Delivery Additional Information The Logistics Model for both Inbound Delivery and Outbound Delivery is set to "Without Tasks."

entry.title: Re: Comparison of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition) vs. SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel

entry.title: Extract Salesforce data into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Overview

To achieve end-to-end business process transformation, we at Signavio want to cover the whole process and IT  landscape of our customers including SAP and also non-SAP systems.

I am happy to share the release of our out-of-the-box content for Salesforce connectivity to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence that you can use to perform process mining analysis on Salesforce processes including Order-to-Cash.

We will go through where you can find the content and how to use it.

Check out content in SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Let's have a look on how you can get started.

The content is now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. On the top left corner under "Explore", view the product "SAP Signavio":

You will see on overview of all APIs and integration packages available for SAP Signavio. This includes the newly released OData API in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence under the tab "API". Check out this blog to learn more. Go to the tab "Integration":

Now you can see all available integration packages for SAP Signavio. You can now select the integration package "Salesforce Integration to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence":

Once you enter the integration package, you will see the individual integration flows. Each integration flow has its purposes with the main goal to replicate business objects and the events of the business objects from Salesforce to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. Hence, as an example, you will see a dedicated integration flow to replicate the business object "Lead" and a dedicated integration flow to replicate its events called "LeadHistory":

If you go into one specific integration flow, you will be able to see the details of the integration flow on how each step of transformation, routing, data mapping, etc. is performed. You can also download the configuration guide that details out how to deploy this integration package in your SAP Integration Suite tenant as well as view the change log for changes between released versions:

Download content into SAP Integration Suite

Now if you want to use the content, log on to SAP Integration Suite and go to "Integrations" under the tab "Discover":

If you now search for "Salesforce", you will find all available content relating to Salesforce integration, including our new package for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence:

If you check out the package "Salesforce Integration with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence", you can additional details on the integration scenarios and which objects are included out-of-the-box. If you now want to use this package, simply click on "copy" on the top right corner to copy this integration package into your design area for further configuration and deployment.

And that is it, you did it! Let me know if you have any questions on the package and please also any additional non-SAP systems that are relevant for you to connect to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 🙂

Here some helpful links to get you started:

SAP Signavio on SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Content: Salesforce Integration with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

entry.title: SAP B1 - Sales Order text line interface back to Delivery Note

Any advise? logically when interface back to SAP B1 Delivery Note by reference back to SO , shouldn't it takes all details of the SO to be populated to  Delivery Note

Thanks in Advance.

With the 2408 update, isn't it possible to perform a Stock Transfer to a blocked Logistics Area? I have an obligation to verify the details of this update and explain it to the customer. Could you please provide me with step-by-step instructions on how to verify the update, along with addressing the above question?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP : Postgres on Azure
entry.content:
Hi mani_ac,
You can locally try by configuring your service bindings (See https://pages.github.tools.sap/cap/docs/guides/databases-postgres#in-your-private-cdsrc-json).
Best regards,
Lars Lutz

entry.title: Re: UDF in Landed Costs is not showing up in Service Layer
entry.content:
Hi Maria_Bocaranda,

Probably, the UDFs were not exposed in the version and patch which you are using.

I can see that the UDFs are there in the latest patch (10.0 FP2405).

Therefore, I would ask you to upgrade.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: UI5 - Usage of Visual Changes in SAP Fiori Elements
entry.content:
Hi @RaminS ,

While manually creating the files, you can surely name them as you please - e.g.:


Best regards,
Joaquim Perez

entry.title: can Datasphere create temporal joins
entry.content:
i wish to create a temporal join, similar to that i have done in HANA Studio.

can this be done in Datasphere?

See image

entry.title: SAP B1 - Create adjustment via API with latest pricing
entry.content:
Hi All,

While interface back to SAP B1 inventory adjustment via API, the pricing is always 0. is there any configuration in SAP B1 that can archive whenever there is a adjustment, just follow the latest item pricing.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: CAP - CDS REPL to connect to db {kind : hana}
entry.content:
Hi Dinu,

Here is the output - 

> cds.env.profiles
[ 'development' ]
>
> cds.env.requires.db
{
  impl: '@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/hana/Service.js',
  use: [Getter],
  model: [ 'db', 'srv' ],
  connection_attempts: 3,
  kind: 'hana'
}

 Thank you.

entry.title: Data Removal
entry.content: How can we easily remove data from one Business Partner field in a large number of records.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Preview of the App showing all the columns, search and filter options in the CDS view entity:

Search records based on a specific value from 'Description' field and select an available value from the 'Status' field.
